idea.topic: The Speed of Light and Time
idea.hook: You never see anything in the present moment.
facts: Light travels at a finite speed: exactly 299,792,458 meters per second.
Sunlight takes 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth. You see the Sun as it was 8 minutes ago.
Light from the Andromeda Galaxy takes 2.5 million years to reach us. You see it as it was 2.5 MYA!
idea.payoff: Every time you look up, you're looking into the universe's past.
idea.title: You're ALWAYS looking into the past! 🤯🌌

scenes.text: You never see anything in the present moment.
scenes.visual: A clock face subtly rippling or blurring, abstractly representing time passing.

scenes.text: Light speed: 299,792,458 m/s. It's not instant.
scenes.visual: A swift beam of light crossing the screen, with the speed number appearing dynamically alongside it.

scenes.text: Sunlight takes 8m 20s. You see the Sun 8 min ago.
scenes.visual: A clear visual of the Sun with an arc of light extending to Earth. A timer overlay shows '8:20'.

scenes.text: Andromeda's light: 2.5 Million Yrs. You see it 2.5 MYA!
scenes.visual: A stunning, detailed image of the Andromeda Galaxy, with a subtle '2.5 MYA' timeline indicator.

scenes.text: Every time you look up, you're looking into the universe's past.
scenes.visual: A vast, breathtaking view of a star field and distant galaxies, slowly panning upwards as if from a human perspective.

youtube.title: You're ALWAYS looking into the past! 🤯🌌
